Advisor.title: Advisor
Advisor.description: Represents a frontline sales advisor within the automotive dealership.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Advisor entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the sales advisor.

phoneNumber.description: The mobile phone number of the advisor, used for OTP login.

employeeId.description: The unique employee ID for the advisor, an alternative login method.

dealershipName.description: The name of the dealership the advisor is associated with.

city.description: The city where the dealership is located.

professionalRole.description: The professional role of the advisor, e.g., 'Sales Manager', 'Sales Consultant'.

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL to the advisor's profile photo.

languagePreference.description: The preferred language for the advisor's app interface.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the advisor's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the advisor's profile was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a potential or existing customer interacting with a sales advisor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor who is managing this customer. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N Customer)

fullName.description: The full name of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

heatStatus.description: The lead's heat status indicating their level of interest.

buyerType.description: Automatically detected buyer persona based on discovery data, e.g., 'Urban Family Buyer', 'First-Time Buyer', 'Safety-Conscious Buyer'.

leadStage.description: The current stage of the customer in the sales funnel, e.g., 'Discovery', 'Test Drive', 'Negotiation'.

vehicleInterestModel.description: The specific vehicle model the customer is interested in. (References Vehicle.modelName)

vehicleInterestTrim.description: The preferred trim level for the interested vehicle.

vehicleInterestColor.description: The preferred color for the interested vehicle.

preferredDeliveryPoint.description: The customer's preferred location for vehicle handover.

decisionMatrix.description: A list of customer priorities and their scores out of ten, influencing their decision.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents an individual interaction session between an advisor and a customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Session entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer involved in this session. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Session)

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor conducting this session. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N Session)

interactionType.description: The type of interaction, e.g., walk-in, phone call, video call, revisit.

status.description: The current status of the session, e.g., 'active', 'completed'.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the session officially started.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the session was marked as completed.

Discovery.title: Discovery
Discovery.description: Stores the results of the 6P customer discovery flow for a specific session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Discovery entity.

sessionId.description: Reference to the Session during which this discovery was conducted. (Relationship: Session 1:1 Discovery)

p1Purpose.description: JSON object storing the advisor's input for the 'Purpose' (P1) step of the 6P discovery.

p2People.description: JSON object storing the advisor's input for the 'People' (P2) step of the 6P discovery.

p3Pattern.description: JSON object storing the advisor's input for the 'Pattern' (P3) step of the 6P discovery.

p4Preferences.description: JSON object storing the advisor's input for the 'Preferences' (P4) step of the 6P discovery.

p5Price.description: JSON object storing the advisor's input for the 'Price' (P5) step of the 6P discovery.

p6PurchaseContext.description: JSON object storing the advisor's input for the 'Purchase Context' (P6) step of the 6P discovery.

generatedPersona.description: The AI-generated buyer persona label based on the 6P data.

advisorBriefing.description: The AI-generated two-line advisor briefing (lead with, highlight, mention, avoid).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the discovery record was created.

CustomerJourneyEvent.title: CustomerJourneyEvent
CustomerJourneyEvent.description: Records individual events and interactions in a customer's journey.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerJourneyEvent entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer associated with this journey event. (Relationship: Customer 1:N CustomerJourneyEvent)

sessionId.description: Reference to the Session in which this event occurred. (Relationship: Session 1:N CustomerJourneyEvent)

eventType.description: The type of event in the customer's journey, e.g., 'walk-in', 'needs-assessment', 'test-drive', 'negotiation', 'follow-up'.

eventDescription.description: A detailed description of the journey event.

aiInsight.description: An AI-generated insight or observation related to this journey event.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the journey event was recorded.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Stores details and metadata for documents uploaded for a customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer this document belongs to. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Document)

fileName.description: The name of the uploaded file.

fileUrl.description: URL where the document is stored.

fileSize.description: The size of the file in bytes.

status.description: The verification status of the document, e.g., 'verified', 'review-in-progress'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Stores textual or voice notes related to a customer interaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Note entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer this note is about. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Note)

authorId.description: Reference to the Advisor who created this note. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N Note)

bodyText.description: The textual content of the note, or a transcript if it was a voice note.

voiceNoteUrl.description: URL to the audio file if the note is a voice note.

voiceNoteDuration.description: The duration of the voice note in seconds.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the note was created.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Stores comprehensive data for each of the four specific vehicle models.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

modelName.description: The unique model name of the vehicle.

tagline.description: A short, catchy phrase summarizing the vehicle's appeal.

startingPrice.description: The starting price of the vehicle model.

variants.description: A JSON array describing different variants (trims) of the vehicle, including names, positioning, prices, and price deltas.

specs.description: A JSON object storing key specifications and features of the vehicle, e.g., mileage, boot space, airbags.

pitchLevel1.description: The AI-generated script for the Functional Pitch level.

pitchLevel2.description: The AI-generated script for the Emotional Pitch level.

pitchLevel3.description: The AI-generated script for the Aspirational Pitch level.

objections.description: A JSON array of common objections for this vehicle with recommended responses.

competitorComparisons.description: A JSON array detailing comparisons against key competitor vehicles.

targetPersona.description: A JSON object describing the ideal target persona for this vehicle, including segment, age range, lifestyle tags, and a descriptor quote.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the vehicle data was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vehicle data was last updated.

TrainingModule.title: TrainingModule
TrainingModule.description: Defines a single training course, live session, or simulation available in the Kinetic Training Hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TrainingModule entity.

title.description: The title of the training module.

category.description: The category of the training module, e.g., 'Product Knowledge', 'Soft Skills'.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the training module in minutes.

type.description: The type of training module, e.g., 'course', 'live', 'simulation'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the training module was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the training module was last updated.

AdvisorPerformance.title: AdvisorPerformance
AdvisorPerformance.description: Records an advisor's aggregated performance metrics for a specific month.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdvisorPerformance entity.

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor whose performance is being tracked. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N AdvisorPerformance)

month.description: The month for which the performance data is recorded (e.g., 'YYYY-MM').

walkInsCount.description: The total count of customer walk-ins handled by the advisor in the month.

conversionsCount.description: The total count of successful conversions (deals closed) by the advisor in the month.

avgDealValue.description: The average value of deals closed by the advisor in the month.

rank.description: The advisor's ranking among peers for the month.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this performance record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this performance record was last updated.

Accessory.title: Accessory
Accessory.description: Represents an individual vehicle accessory available for deal configuration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Accessory entity.

description.description: A brief description of the accessory.

category.description: The category the accessory belongs to, e.g., 'Essential', 'Protection and Care', 'Tech and Infotainment'.

isRecommended.description: Indicates if the accessory is AI-recommended.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the accessory record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the accessory record was last updated.

DealConfiguration.title: DealConfiguration
DealConfiguration.description: Represents a specific vehicle deal being configured for a customer, including selected vehicle and accessories.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DealConfiguration entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer for whom this deal is configured. (Relationship: Customer 1:N DealConfiguration)

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor who configured this deal. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N DealConfiguration)

vehicleId.description: Reference to the selected Vehicle for this deal. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N DealConfiguration)

selectedTrim.description: The specific trim level chosen for the vehicle.

selectedColor.description: The chosen color for the vehicle.

basePrice.description: The base ex-showroom price of the selected vehicle configuration.

selectedAccessoryIds.description: References to the selected Accessory entities included in this deal. (Relationship: Accessory N:N DealConfiguration)

accessoriesTotal.description: The total price of all selected accessories.

finalPrice.description: The final calculated price of the deal, including vehicle and accessories.

status.description: The status of the deal configuration, e.g., 'draft', 'confirmed', 'converted'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the deal configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the deal configuration was last updated.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents an individual notification for an advisor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor to whom this notification is directed. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N Notification)

body.description: The main content text of the notification.

icon.description: An identifier or URL for the icon associated with the notification type.

actionType.description: The type of action associated with the notification, e.g., 'ViewLead', 'OpenTargets'.

actionTargetId.description: Optional: ID of the entity that the action should navigate to (e.g., customerId, trainingModuleId).

isRead.description: Indicates whether the advisor has read the notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was generated.

AdvisorTrainingProgress.title: AdvisorTrainingProgress
AdvisorTrainingProgress.description: Tracks an individual advisor's progress on a specific training module.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdvisorTrainingProgress entity.

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor whose training progress is being tracked. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N AdvisorTrainingProgress)

trainingModuleId.description: Reference to the TrainingModule being undertaken. (Relationship: TrainingModule 1:N AdvisorTrainingProgress)

completionPercentage.description: The percentage of the training module completed by the advisor (0-100).

status.description: The current status of the advisor's progress, e.g., 'not-started', 'in-progress', 'completed'.

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp when the advisor last accessed this training module.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the advisor completed the training module.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this training progress record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this training progress record was last updated.

SimulatorScenario.title: SimulatorScenario
SimulatorScenario.description: Defines a single co-pilot simulation scenario for advisor training.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SimulatorScenario entity.

scenarioDescription.description: A description of the simulation context and objective.

salesGoal.description: The primary sales goal for this simulation scenario.

customerStatement.description: The customer's statement or objection presented in the simulation.

responseOptions.description: A JSON array of possible response options for the advisor to choose from.

feedback.description: A JSON object containing AI-generated feedback for a chosen response, including coaching message, explanation, and performance metrics.

audioFeedbackUrl.description: URL to an optional audio feedback recording for the scenario.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the simulation scenario was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the simulation scenario was last updated.

AdvisorSimulationAttempt.title: AdvisorSimulationAttempt
AdvisorSimulationAttempt.description: Records an advisor's attempt at completing a simulation scenario and their performance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdvisorSimulationAttempt entity.

advisorId.description: Reference to the Advisor who attempted the simulation. (Relationship: Advisor 1:N AdvisorSimulationAttempt)

simulatorScenarioId.description: Reference to the SimulatorScenario that was attempted. (Relationship: SimulatorScenario 1:N AdvisorSimulationAttempt)

chosenResponse.description: The text of the response option chosen by the advisor.

performanceScores.description: A JSON object containing the advisor's scores for need identification, product mapping, and closing.

coachingMessage.description: The AI-generated coaching message provided after the attempt.

auraAiFeedback.description: The general AI feedback provided for the simulation attempt.

attemptedAt.description: Timestamp when the simulation attempt occurred.

definition.description: Stores individual advisor profiles. Each advisor's data is private to them. The 'advisorId' in the path matches 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Stores monthly performance metrics for a specific advisor. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: performanceId
params.description: The unique ID for the performance record (e.g., YYYY-MM).

definition.description: Stores notifications directed to a specific advisor. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique ID for the notification.

definition.description: Tracks an advisor's progress on training modules. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: progressId
params.description: The unique ID for the training progress record.

definition.description: Records an advisor's attempt at a simulation scenario. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique ID for the simulation attempt record.

definition.description: Stores customer records, privately owned and managed by a specific advisor. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

definition.description: Records individual interaction sessions between an advisor and a customer. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' and 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the interaction session.

definition.description: Stores the 6P customer discovery flow results for a specific session. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' and 'customerId' (implicitly via path, explicitly in document for rule efficiency) for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the interaction session.

definition.description: Records individual events in a customer's journey. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' and 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID for the customer journey event.

definition.description: Stores details and metadata for documents uploaded for a customer. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' and 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique ID for the document.

definition.description: Stores notes related to a customer interaction. Includes denormalized 'authorId' (advisorId) and 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique ID for the note.

definition.description: Represents a vehicle deal configured for a customer. Includes denormalized 'advisorId' and 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: advisorId
params.description: The unique ID of the advisor (matches Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer.

params.name: dealConfigId
params.description: The unique ID for the deal configuration.

definition.description: Stores static, read-only data for each of the four specific vehicle models, accessible to all authenticated advisors. No denormalized 'advisorId' required as this is shared data.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique ID for the vehicle model.

definition.description: Stores static, read-only data for vehicle accessories, accessible to all authenticated advisors. No denormalized 'advisorId' required as this is shared data.

params.name: accessoryId
params.description: The unique ID for the accessory.

definition.description: Defines static, read-only training courses and simulations, accessible to all authenticated advisors. No denormalized 'advisorId' required as this is shared data.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique ID for the training module.

definition.description: Defines static, read-only co-pilot simulation scenarios, accessible to all authenticated advisors. No denormalized 'advisorId' required as this is shared data.

params.name: scenarioId
params.description: The unique ID for the simulator scenario.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to rigorously adhere to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and the use of Hierarchical Paths for user-owned data. The primary goal is to facilitate simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules while ensuring data integrity and query efficiency.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
This is achieved by denormalizing critical authorization fields into every document where access might otherwise depend on a parent document. For instance, `advisorId` is embedded not just in the top-level `Advisor` document, but also in `Customer`, `Session`, `Discovery`, `CustomerJourneyEvent`, `Document`, `Note`, `DealConfiguration`, `AdvisorPerformance`, `Notification`, `AdvisorTrainingProgress`, and `AdvisorSimulationAttempt` documents. This allows security rules to evaluate access permissions solely based on the `request.auth.uid` matching the `advisorId` within the document being accessed, or the `advisorId` wildcard in the path, without needing expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents. For example, a rule for a customer's note can directly check `request.auth.uid == /advisors/{advisorId}/customers/{customerId}/notes/{noteId}.data.advisorId` or `request.auth.uid == advisorId` from the path wildcard, eliminating external lookups.

To perfectly align with this, the `Discovery`, `CustomerJourneyEvent`, and `Document` entities will need an explicit `advisorId` field added to their schema, as it's not explicitly listed in the provided schema for these three entities but is crucial for Authorization Independence within their nested paths.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**
All data related to an individual advisor, and the customers they manage, is organized under a top-level `/advisors/{advisorId}/` collection. This creates a secure data silo for each advisor. For example:
*   `/advisors/{advisorId}/customers/{customerId}`
*   `/advisors/{advisorId}/customers/{customerId}/sessions/{sessionId}`
*   `/advisors/{advisorId}/notifications/{notificationId}`

This hierarchical structure ensures that `list` queries (QAPs) are inherently secure. An advisor querying `firestore.collection('advisors').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('customers')` will only retrieve *their* customers. The security rules can simply state `allow list: if request.auth.uid == advisorId;`, which is highly efficient and secure as Firestore filters data based on the path. Mixing data with different security postures is avoided; private advisor/customer data is segregated from public, read-only application data.

Public, static application data like `Vehicles`, `Accessories`, `TrainingModules`, and `SimulatorScenarios` are stored in root-level collections (e.g., `/vehicles/{vehicleId}`). These collections will have security rules allowing `read` access to all authenticated users (`if request.auth != null`), but no write access for advisors, ensuring data integrity for these static assets. This segregation simplifies rules, making them easier to audit and maintain.

**Consistency and Debuggability:**
Wildcards are consistently descriptive (e.g., `{advisorId}`, `{customerId}`). Fields like `status` are used for explicit state modeling. The consistent use of `advisorId` throughout ensures clear intent for ownership and access. This approach minimizes complexity in security rules, making them easier to write, understand, and debug.
